title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play effectiveness has plummeted to 12.8%, ranking 28th in the NHL since the Olympic break.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have contributed no 5-on-5 points in the Bruins' 11 games post-Olympics.
Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov have been outscored 4-2 in 5-on-5 play since the team returned from Milano-Cortina.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit a crucial home run to give Venezuela a 2-0 lead in the World Baseball Classic final.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez tossed over four scoreless innings, aiding Venezuela's 3-2 win for their first title.
Red Sox's Garrett Whitlock allowed the winning run for Venezuela in the ninth inning, ending Team USA's World Baseball Classic run.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan, 39, from Ashland, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
Flanagan began running three years ago, finding it beneficial for her mental health and overall well-being.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical needs and offers relief to their families.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted of manslaughter in death of 10-month-old foster child in Adams.
The child died from untreated strep infection, bronchopneumonia, and empyema, conditions treatable with timely medical care.
The couple faces up to 20 years for manslaughter and additional time for child endangerment, sentencing set for March 19.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A water main break at Columbus Avenue and Centre and Ritchie streets flooded Jackson Square Tuesday.
The MBTA rerouted bus routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 due to the Jackson Square flood.
Crews worked to shut off the 36-inch transmission main affecting a large area including Egleston Square.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle traded to the Broncos for three picks, including a 2026 first-rounder, impacting the Patriots' strategies.
The trade boosts the Broncos' roster, strengthening their position in the AFC against contenders like the Patriots.
Denver's trade for Waddle raises the potential cost for the Patriots to acquire Eagles' A.J. Brown.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, were charged after a Jeep hit three pedestrians outside ICON nightclub on Feb. 28.
Boyce faces charges including felony assault, firearm storage violation, and reckless driving; Moraes is charged with interfering and disorderly conduct.
The pair allegedly exited the vehicle to confront victims and reportedly stole items before being identified and stopped by police.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made a surprise appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick's Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden's speech highlighted his Irish heritage and compared historical and current immigrant challenges in the U.S.
Governor Maura Healey joined Biden in addressing immigration issues, emphasizing shared immigrant ancestry experiences.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured in a deck collapse at a house party in Brighton.
The collapse involved a railing breaking off, causing students to fall from the second-floor deck.
Police found the wood rotting and painted over, with corroded nails, concealing the damage.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots rookie safety Craig Woodson earned a $1,149,910 performance bonus for his contributions during the 2025 season.
Woodson led the Patriots' defense with 950 snaps and recorded 79 tackles, three pass breakups, and two fumble recoveries.
In the postseason, Woodson excelled with 30 tackles and six pass breakups, contributing significantly in Super Bowl LX against Seattle.
